questions.question: What is the target weekly time budget for customer success activities for a solo founder?
questions.explanation: The target is 5-7 hours per week — roughly 20-25% of productive time. If CS consumes more than this, acquisition and product work suffer. If you're spending more than 7 hours with fewer than 100 customers, you have a systems problem.

questions.question: How should the 5-7 hour CS budget be allocated across activities?
questions.explanation: The recommended split is: Onboarding 30% (1-2 hours), Retention 40% (2-3 hours), and Expansion 30% (1-2 hours). Everything else gets automated or deflected. This ensures a balance of acquiring value, protecting value, and growing value.

questions.question: Instead of reacting to support requests throughout the day, what approach does the lesson recommend?

options.text: Batch all CS work into three focused blocks per week
Only respond to support tickets on weekends
Set up an AI chatbot to handle everything

questions.explanation: Batch all CS work into three focused blocks: Monday morning scan (90 min), Wednesday execution (90 min), and Friday review (60 min). This is the single most effective time management shift for solo founders doing CS.

questions.question: Making yourself available 24/7 for customer support is the best strategy for solo founder retention.
questions.explanation: False. Availability without boundaries doesn't scale and leads to burnout. It ironically makes you less effective at helping customers succeed. The solution is designing a CS system that delivers exceptional outcomes in structured time blocks.

questions.question: Describe your current weekly CS time allocation. How many hours do you spend, and on what activities? Design your ideal 3-block CS week, specifying what you would do in each block (Monday scan, Wednesday execution, Friday review).
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response honestly assesses current time spent (likely 8-15+ hours), identifies what's consuming time unnecessarily, and designs a concrete 3-block schedule with specific activities per block that totals 5-7 hours. Look for understanding that the shift is from reactive to proactive.
